name: World
description: which discusses broad global history and worldwide trends, offering comparative narratives across regions and long time spans, and often reviewed alongside books covering globalization, world systems, and cross-cultural historical analysis.

name: Americas
description: which focuses on the history of North, Central, and South America, addressing colonization, independence, social change, and national development, and commonly associated with books on regional politics, culture, and historical identity.

name: Asia
description: which examines Asian history and civilizations, covering dynasties, religions, traditions, and modernization, and frequently grouped with books discussing cultural continuity, regional interaction, and long-term social development.

name: Military
description: which centers on wars, conflicts, and military strategy, emphasizing campaigns, leadership, and technology, and often co-reviewed with books analyzing major battles, defense systems, and the societal consequences of warfare.

name: Europe
description: which explores European history through empires, political transformation, intellectual movements, and social change, and is commonly linked to books on state formation, revolutions, and modern European identity.

name: Russia
description: which focuses on Russian history across imperial, Soviet, and post-Soviet periods, highlighting power, ideology, and regional influence, and frequently associated with books on Cold War history, authoritarian governance, and geopolitical strategy.

name: Africa
description: which examines African history with attention to indigenous societies, colonial rule, resistance, and post-colonial development, and is often grouped with books addressing cultural heritage, inequality, and modern political change.

name: Ancient Civilizations
description: which discusses early civilizations and ancient societies, focusing on origins, archaeology, governance, and belief systems, and commonly reviewed alongside books on early states, ancient cultures, and the foundations of human history.

name: Middle East
description: which explores Middle Eastern history across ancient, medieval, and modern periods, emphasizing religion, empire, conflict, and geopolitics, and frequently associated with books on regional power, belief systems, and contemporary political issues.

name: Historical Study & Educational Resources
description: which provides historical overviews, explanations, and learning materials, emphasizing interpretation and method, and often linked to textbooks, reference works, and introductory guides for students and general readers.

name: Australia & Oceania
description: which focuses on the history of Australia, New Zealand, and Pacific regions, covering indigenous societies, exploration, colonization, and modern development, and commonly grouped with books on regional identity and colonial experience.

name: Arctic & Antarctica
description: which explores polar history and exploration, emphasizing expeditions, science, and extreme environments, and often associated with books on discovery, environmental change, and geopolitical interest in polar regions.
